---
title: "This may be problem with i845 on ubuntu 8.10"
date: 2008-12-21
forum: General Help
---

### Post by ravi.xolve on 2008-12-21
I think I identified the cause of the problem faced by the intel 845 graphics controllers on ubuntu 8.10 . Following is xorg.conf file for i845 graphics controller.

```

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#                                                            
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# Note that some configuration settings that could be done previously
# in this file, now are automatically configured by the server and settings
# here are ignored.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "Configured Video Device"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "Configured Monitor"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Default Screen"
        Monitor         "Configured Monitor"
        Device          "Configured Video Device"
EndSection
```

This means xserver leaves the xorg.conf file unconfigured. Can anybody with intel 845 graphics controller post the xorg.conf file here so that we may give it a try and see if we can get kwin effects (or compiz effects). I don't know how to write xorg.conf file from scratch :(

---

### Post by monkeyking on 2008-12-21
What's the problem with the xorg.conf

### Post by densou on 2008-12-21
yeah, many 2008's distros had trouble regarding auto-detecting Intel video cards (it made me angry when I updated to hardy from gutsy) 
and Intrepid came with an old 'libdrm', which prevents to install newer Intel releases.

first, install these packages (ask me if you have trouble reading this page):
[https://edge.launchpad.net/~intel-gfx-testing/+archive](https://edge.launchpad.net/~intel-gfx-testing/+archive)

then read how I set up my xorg:
[http://spazioinwind.libero.it/densou/xorg.conf.new](http://spazioinwind.libero.it/densou/xorg.conf.new)

it works fine, while with hardy I had to write too many stuff (have a look: [http://spazioinwind.libero.it/densou/xorg.conf.ibex](http://spazioinwind.libero.it/densou/xorg.conf.ibex) )

note: I'd suggest to edit also /etc/modules [type 'sudo gedit /etc/modules/ in a new prompt window]; you must have these lines --> agpgart 
intel-agp 
drm
if not, just add them! :)

---

### Post by ravi.xolve on 2008-12-22
@densou, before I do the job I want to ask, were you able to get kwin and/or compiz effects back?

---

### Post by densou on 2008-12-22
well, according to this [page]("https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/yelp/+bug/259385") it seems you should give up with Intrepid+Compiz
My parents' desktop has a 82945G/GL (GMA950 known as 'i945'), I have deactivated Compiz for daily use, but if I enable it again, it works smooth (w/ no boot issue) :-k



Note: I fixed URLs on my previous post, now my xorg.conf can be read :)

---

### Post by ravi.xolve on 2008-12-29
Thanks densou

I couldn't get the kwin effects :(
But the system performance improved due to faster graphics.

### Post by ravi.xolve on 2009-04-18
With the xorg config files you provided and this post in the bug reports [https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/yelp/+bug/259385/comments/133](https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/yelp/+bug/259385/comments/133) I was able to restore Kwin effects. Thanks a lot.

### Post by ravi.xolve on 2009-04-19

Well I feel this is not good. While Ubuntu provides easy interface for propriety nvidia and ati drivers, open source intel ones are not even given proper care. Everything was fine here, it just needed some detection.
